findings.title: ingestcli now has a hidden metrics-init prerequisite and can panic in NextGen callers
findings.why: The new duration observations dereference package-level observers that stay nil until `ingestmetric.InitIngestMetrics()` runs. `ingestcli` itself does not establish that initialization, so the change turns metrics bootstrap order into an implicit runtime contract for both package consumers and the NextGen Lightning path.
findings.risk_if_unchanged: Any focused test or runtime that reaches `pkg/ingestor/ingestcli` without first importing and initializing `pkg/metrics` can panic on the first write or ingest request. In Lightning's NextGen flow that escalates a telemetry-only change into a hard request failure or process crash path.
findings.evidence: `WriteAPIDuration` and `IngestAPIDuration` are zero-value globals until `InitIngestMetrics()` assigns them in `pkg/ingestor/ingestmetric/metric.go:39-49`. The new code now calls `Observe(...)` unconditionally in `pkg/ingestor/ingestcli/client.go:125` and `pkg/ingestor/ingestcli/client.go:273`. The only checked-in initializer is wired through `pkg/metrics.InitMetrics()` in `pkg/metrics/metrics.go:81-102`, while Lightning boots its own registry via `metric.NewMetrics(...); metrics.RegisterTo(o.promRegistry)` in `lightning/pkg/server/lightning.go:447-448` before using `ingestcli.NewClient(...)` from `pkg/lightning/backend/local/local.go:1682`.
findings.change_request: Make `ingestcli` safe without relying on out-of-band `pkg/metrics` side effects. Either initialize default observers inside `ingestmetric`, make the observe sites nil-safe, or explicitly wire these metrics through Lightning's registry before `ingestcli` is used. Please add a focused regression test for the no-`pkg/metrics` bootstrap path.

findings.title: Write-side metric name suggests per-call latency, but the code records one sample for the whole stream
findings.why: The current `WriteAPIDuration` label reads like latency for each `Write(*WriteRequest)` call, but the timer starts in `writeClient.init` and records exactly once when the long-lived chunked PUT completes.
findings.risk_if_unchanged: Dashboards and future instrumentation can mix stream-level latency on the write path with request-level latency on the ingest path, which makes the metric easy to misread and easy to double-count later.
findings.evidence: The write timer starts in `writeClient.init` before `startChunkedHTTPRequest`, is observed once in the goroutine defer, and `TestWriteClientDurationMetricObserveOnce` calls `client.Write(...)` twice while asserting the histogram count only increases by one.
findings.change_request: Rename this metric to something stream- or session-scoped, such as `WriteStreamDuration` or `WriteSessionDuration`, and add a short comment that one observation covers the full chunked write lifecycle rather than each `Write` call.

findings.title: Worker Grafana panel was changed only in the generated JSON, not in its maintained source
findings.why: `tidb_worker.json` is regenerated from `tidb_with_keyspace_name.json`, so editing the generated worker dashboard alone creates a second source of truth and makes the panel addition non-durable.
findings.risk_if_unchanged: The next `generate_json.sh` run will silently overwrite this new row title and panel, so the committed worker dashboard will drift from the checked-in generator input and future dashboard edits will regress unexpectedly.
findings.evidence: `pkg/metrics/nextgengrafana/generate_json.sh:27-31` rebuilds `tidb_worker.json` from `tidb_with_keyspace_name.json`. The PR adds `Write/Ingest API Duration` and renames the row to `Global Sort & Ingest` only in `pkg/metrics/nextgengrafana/tidb_worker.json:25313-25408`, while `pkg/metrics/nextgengrafana/tidb_with_keyspace_name.json:26194-26208` still has only `Active Parallel Upload Worker Count` under the old `Global Sort` row.
findings.change_request: Move this dashboard change into the maintained generator input for the worker dashboard, then regenerate `tidb_worker.json` so the committed JSON stays in sync with its upstream definition.

findings.title: The new metric package exports mutable observer vars without a concrete external need
findings.why: Exporting `WriteAPIDuration` and `IngestAPIDuration` makes initialization order and mutability part of the package contract even though the only non-test caller is the local ingest client.
findings.risk_if_unchanged: Other packages can start depending on these vars directly, which widens the API surface for an internal instrumentation detail and makes later refactors or renames harder.
findings.evidence: A repo-wide search in this worktree shows `WriteAPIDuration` and `IngestAPIDuration` are only referenced from `pkg/ingestor/ingestcli/client.go`, while tests inspect the histogram vec `WriteIngestAPIDuration` rather than the per-label observers.
findings.change_request: Keep the per-label observers private or wrap them in unexported observe helpers, and leave only the minimum surface needed for registration and test introspection.
